questions.q: Rana Sanga was the ruler of which Rajput kingdom?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga, or Maharana Sangram Singh, was the ruler of Mewar in the early 16th century.

questions.q: What was Rana Sanga's formal given name?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga's full name was Maharana Sangram Singh of Mewar.

questions.q: In which battle was Rana Sanga defeated by Babur in 1527?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga was defeated by Babur at the Battle of Khanwa in 1527.

questions.q: Rana Sanga belonged to which Rajput clan of Mewar?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga belonged to the Sisodia clan that ruled Mewar.

questions.q: In which century did Rana Sanga rule Mewar?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga was a formidable Rajput ruler of the early 16th century CE.

questions.q: Against whom did Rana Sanga lead a confederacy of Rajput states at Khanwa?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga led a Rajput confederacy against Babur, the founder of the Mughal Empire.

questions.q: The capital fortress associated with Rana Sanga's Mewar was which famous fort?
questions.explanation: Chittorgarh was the storied capital fortress of the Sisodia rulers of Mewar, including Rana Sanga.

questions.q: What was the outcome of the Battle of Khanwa for Rana Sanga?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga was defeated by Babur at Khanwa in 1527.

questions.q: Rana Sanga is remembered chiefly as a champion of which community?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga is celebrated as a Rajput champion who united several Rajput states.

questions.q: Which title did rulers of Mewar such as Rana Sanga bear?
questions.explanation: The rulers of Mewar held the title Maharana, hence Maharana Sangram Singh.

questions.q: Rana Sanga was famously said to have lost the use of one of which body parts in battle?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Rana Sanga lost an eye, along with an arm and bore many wounds from his battles.

questions.q: In which year was the Battle of Khanwa fought?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Khanwa was fought in 1527, the year after Babur's victory at Panipat.

questions.q: Before facing Babur, Rana Sanga is traditionally credited with defeating the sultans of which two neighbouring sultanates?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga won renown for victories against the sultans of Malwa and Gujarat before Khanwa.

questions.q: Babur declared a jihad before Khanwa and renounced what to rally his troops?
options: Wine and the breaking of wine-cups
questions.explanation: Babur famously renounced wine and broke his drinking vessels to inspire his army before Khanwa.

questions.q: Rana Sanga was the son of which Mewar ruler?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga was a son of Rana Raimal of Mewar and succeeded after a struggle among his brothers.

questions.q: The site of the Battle of Khanwa lies near which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Khanwa is located in present-day Rajasthan, west of Agra.

questions.q: What decisively turned the Battle of Khanwa in Babur's favour, much as at Panipat?
options: A war-elephant charge by his side
questions.explanation: As at Panipat, Babur's artillery and firearms proved decisive against the Rajput cavalry at Khanwa.

questions.q: After his defeat at Khanwa, Rana Sanga reportedly vowed not to do what until he had avenged the loss?
questions.explanation: Tradition says the wounded Rana Sanga vowed not to return to Chittor until he had defeated Babur.

questions.q: Rana Sanga died not long after Khanwa, reportedly in which year?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga died around 1528, shortly after the defeat at Khanwa.

questions.q: Which later, more famous Maharana of Mewar was a descendant of Rana Sanga?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap, who resisted Akbar, was a grandson of Rana Sanga through Udai Singh II.

questions.q: An Afghan claimant Babur defeated at Khanwa-era campaigns, fighting alongside Rajput interests, was Mahmud Lodi, brother of which slain sultan?
questions.explanation: Mahmud Lodi, brother of the slain Ibrahim Lodi, allied with Rana Sanga against Babur.

questions.q: On approximately which date in March 1527 was the Battle of Khanwa fought?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Khanwa was fought on or around 16 March 1527.

questions.q: Tradition holds that Rana Sanga's body bore roughly how many wounds and missing limbs from a lifetime of war?
options: He bore some eighty wounds, having lost an arm and an eye
He had only a single small scar
questions.explanation: Accounts describe Rana Sanga as bearing around eighty wounds, having lost an arm and an eye in battle.

questions.q: After Khanwa, Babur reportedly erected a tower made of what to mark his victory?
questions.explanation: Babur is said to have built a tower of the skulls of slain foes after Khanwa, a grim victory marker.

questions.q: Many accounts state that Rana Sanga was carried unconscious from the field at Khanwa after being struck down; who reportedly led the Rajputs in his place using his royal insignia?
options: A chief who took up Sanga's standard while Sanga was borne away wounded
questions.explanation: When Rana Sanga fell wounded, a chief took up his royal canopy and standard, but the line broke and the Rajputs were routed.

questions.q: Some chronicles allege Rana Sanga's death after Khanwa was hastened by what, rather than battle wounds alone?
options: Poisoning by his own nobles who opposed renewing war with Babur
A fall from a fort wall
questions.explanation: Several chronicles suggest Rana Sanga was poisoned by nobles who feared another disastrous war with Babur, though accounts vary.

questions.q: Rana Sanga's confederacy at Khanwa notably included Hasan Khan Mewati, ruler of which territory?
questions.explanation: Hasan Khan Mewati of Mewat was a key ally of Rana Sanga and fell at Khanwa.

questions.q: Which Rajput chief of Marwar, ancestor of the later Jodhpur line, is associated with Rana Sanga's wider Rajput resurgence of the period?
options: Rao Ganga of Marwar (and his son Maldeo)
questions.explanation: Rao Ganga of Marwar, whose son Maldeo later expanded Marwar, was part of the Rajput political world of Rana Sanga's era.

questions.q: Rana Sanga's reign followed a bitter succession struggle in which he was injured; with which brothers did he contend for the Mewar throne?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga contended with his brothers, including Prithviraj and Jaimal, in the struggle for the Mewar throne.

questions.q: Khanwa is often regarded as more decisive than Panipat for Mughal rule because it removed which threat?
options: The Portuguese on the western coast
The leading indigenous Rajput power that could have expelled Babur from north India
questions.explanation: Khanwa broke the main Rajput coalition under Rana Sanga, the strongest indigenous force that might have driven Babur out, securing Mughal dominance in the north.
